fr:mainmatter.html:p: Hylomorphisms organize into a profunctor \mathrm {Hylo} : \mathrm {Coalg}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}}^{\mathrm {op}} \times \mathrm {Alg}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} \to \mathbf {Set}, sending a coalgebra and an algebra to the set of hylomorphisms between them.

A coalgebra is recursive exactly when \mathrm {Hylo}{\mathopen {}\left (c, -\right )\mathclose {}} is constantly a singleton, and an algebra is corecursive exactly when \mathrm {Hylo}{\mathopen {}\left (-, \alpha \right )\mathclose {}} is. Saying that all values of \mathrm {Hylo} are singletons says every divide-and-conquer specification over F has a unique solution.

fr:title.text: Code locations for the guarded recursion implementation

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Code locations for the guarded recursion implementation

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Where each note lives in cubical-categorical-logic. Module names are relative to the repository root.

html:ul.html:li: Algebras as a displayed category, Initial algebra — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.Algebras
Coalgebras as a displayed category, Terminal coalgebra — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.Coalgebras
The comparison functor, Monadicity and comonadicity — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.EilenbergMoore.Comparison, …CoEilenbergMoore.Comparison
The adjoint triple, Presheaves are monadic and comonadic over families — Cubical.Categories.Presheaf.Family.Base
Well-founded posets are thin direct categories — Cubical.Categories.Direct.Instances.Poset, …Instances.Nat
Sieves, Proper and maximal sieves — Cubical.Categories.Presheaf.Sieve, Cubical.Categories.Subobject.Base
The strict downset, its maximality, Later on presheaves, Löb induction for presheaves, Later on families, Löb induction on families — Cubical.Categories.Direct.StrictDownset
Locally contractive endofunctors, Local contractivity makes every coalgebra recursive — Cubical.Categories.Direct.LocallyContractive
Hylomorphisms, Recursive coalgebras, The coalgebra-to-algebra profunctor, Unique fixed points — Cubical.Categories.Displayed.Instances.Algebras.Recursive
Selective ordered semirings — Cubical.Algebra.OrderedSemiring and its Selective, MinPlus, Bool submodules

fr:title.text: Local contractivity makes every coalgebra recursive

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Local contractivity makes every coalgebra recursive

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If F is a locally contractive endofunctor on presheaves over a direct category, then every value of the profunctor \mathrm {Hylo} is a singleton: every coalgebra is recursive and every algebra is corecursive.

The proof is one application of Löb. A hylomorphism from c to \alpha is a fixed point of h \mapsto c \star F h \star \alpha on the presheaf X \Rightarrow B of morphisms. Local contractivity factors this assignment through \rhd {\mathopen {}\left (X \Rightarrow B\right )\mathclose {}}, so Löb provides a fixed point and provides it uniquely.

A coalgebra c : X \to F X is recursive when for every algebra \alpha : F B \to B there is exactly one hylomorphism X \to B. Recursion out of X is then total and deterministic: any way of recombining answers yields a uniquely-defined function.

Recursiveness is the coalgebraic form of well-foundedness. The coalgebra decomposes each element into subproblems; recursiveness says this decomposition terminates, without ever mentioning an ordering. Alexandru, Urbat, and Wißmann develop intrinsically correct algorithms from exactly this notion.

Dually, an algebra \alpha is corecursive when every coalgebra admits exactly one hylomorphism into it. An initial algebra gives a recursive coalgebra (its inverse, by Lambek's lemma), and a terminal coalgebra gives a corecursive algebra.
This is divide and conquer as an equation: c splits a problem into an F-shape of subproblems, F h solves them, and \alpha recombines the answers.

Fix an endofunctor F : \mathcal {C} \to \mathcal {C}. Coalgebras require no new construction: a coalgebra is an algebra in the opposite category. Define \mathrm {CoalgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} = \mathrm {AlgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F^{\mathrm {op}}\right )\mathclose {}}, a displayed category over \mathcal {C}^{\mathrm {op}}, where F^{\mathrm {op}} : \mathcal {C}^{\mathrm {op}} \to \mathcal {C}^{\mathrm {op}} is F acting on the opposite category.
Concretely, over an object x a displayed object is a structure map \gamma \in \mathcal {C} {\mathopen {}\left (x , F x\right )\mathclose {}}.

The category of coalgebras is the opposite of the total category: \mathrm {Coalg}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} = {\mathopen {}\left (\int \mathrm {CoalgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}}\right )\mathclose {}}^{\mathrm {op}}. The outer opposite returns morphisms to the direction of \mathcal {C}: a morphism {\mathopen {}\left (x, \gamma \right )\mathclose {}} \to {\mathopen {}\left (y, \delta \right )\mathclose {}} is a map f : x \to y with f \star \delta = \gamma \star F f.
Fix an endofunctor F : \mathcal {C} \to \mathcal {C}. The F-algebras form a displayed category \mathrm {AlgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} over \mathcal {C}.
The total category \mathrm {Alg}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} = \int \mathrm {AlgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} is the category of F-algebras.
